zh.lifeSummary: 當Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin於大約1165年出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 薩辛可萊斯 比特費爾德布雷納時，他的父親Friedrich von Brehna I24歲，母親Hedvika Přemyslovna12歲。他和Jutta von Ziegenhain至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1221年10月23日享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Brehna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Lombardy): habitational name from a place in Lombardy named Brenna.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farms in southeastern Norway named Brenna, from brenna ‘land cleared for cultivation by burning’.

variants.name: Brena
meanings: Italian (Lombardy, in province of Bergamo): variant of   Brenna  .
Norwegian: variant of   Brenna  .

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Otto, Wilhelm.

English: from the Middle English personal name Mund(e), in many cases representing Old English Munda, a pet form of names like Ēadmund (Middle English Edmund, common in East Anglia). In northern and eastern England the equivalent Old Norse Mundi (a pet form of names like Agmundr) would also have given rise to Middle English Mund(e).
German: variant of   Mundt  .

variants.name: Monday
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal name Mundi, Mundy, usually a pet form of male personal names ending in -mund or -mond (see Mund , and compare Edmond , Hammond , and Simmonds ). Alternatively, and especially in northern and eastern England, the name might derive from Old Norse Mundi, a pet form of names like Old Norse Agmundr, Middle English Agemund.
nickname from Middle English Mone(n)day ‘Monday’. It is possibly a name given to one born on that day or to a holder of Mondayland, land held on condition of working for the lord on Mondays.

variants.name: Mundt
meanings: German:
nickname for someone with a prominent or striking mouth or whose mouth was in some other way remarkable, from Middle High German munt ‘mouth’.
nickname for a guardian or trustworthy person, from Middle High German munt ‘guardian, protector’.

variants.name: Munson
meanings: English (mainly Essex): patronymic from the personal name   Mund  , meaning ‘son of Mund’. See also   Munn  .
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish   Monsen  . Compare   Munsen  .
Altered form of   Monson   1, a surname of Swedish origin.

nameMeanings.name: Wettin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Johan, Tor.
Swedish: ornamental or habitational name formed with an altered form of the (placename) element väst ‘west’ + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’. Compare Westen .

variants.name: Westen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shosh, Shoshana.  German Ulrich.

German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German, Middle Low German, Middle Dutch westen, German Westen ‘west’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a settlement, or a habitational name for one who had migrated from further west. The Jewish name may also be of ornamental origin.

Swedish (mainly Westén): ornamental or habitational name formed with an altered form of the (placename) element väst ‘west’ + the adjectival suffix -én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’. Compare Westin .

nameMeanings.name: Friedrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

variants.name: Frederick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Friederich
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: Jonathan
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘God has given’, composed of the same elements as those of   Matthew  , but in reverse order. This is the name of several characters in the Bible, most notably a son of King Saul, who was a devoted friend and supporter of the young David, even when David and Saul were themselves at loggerheads (1 Samuel 31; 2 Samuel 1:19–26). The name is often taken as symbolic of steadfast friendship and loyalty. Well-known bearers of the name include the Irish clergyman and writer  Jonathan Swift  (  1667-1745 ), British theatre director  Jonathan Miller  (  b. 1934 ), British actor  Jonathan Pryce  (  b. 1947 ), and British television presenter  Jonathan Ross  (  b. 1960 ).

variants.name: Jonn
meanings: Variant spelling of Jon or John .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin was born about 1165, in Brehna, Kreis Bitterfeld, Province of Saxony, Prussia, Germany, his father, Friedrich von Brehna I, was 24 and his mother, Hedvika Přemyslovna, was 12. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Jutta von Ziegenhain. He died on 23 October 1221, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin est né environ 1165, à Brehna, Kreis Bitterfeld, Provinz Sachsen, Prusse, Allemagne, son père, Friedrich von Brehna I, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Hedvika Přemyslovna, avait 12 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Jutta von Ziegenhain. Il est décédé le 23 octobre 1221, à l’âge de 57 ans.

Er hatte mit Jutta von Ziegenhain mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Oktober 1221 im Alter von 57 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin è nato il circa 1165 nel Brehna, Kreis Bitterfeld, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, Germania, il suo padre, Friedrich von Brehna I, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Hedvika Přemyslovna, aveva 12 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Jutta von Ziegenhain. Morì il 23 ottobre 1221, all'età di 57 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin(이)가 대략 1165년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 작센 크레이스 비터펠드 브레h나에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Friedrich von Brehna I(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Hedvika Přemyslovna(은)는 12세였다. 그는 Jutta von Ziegenhain(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1221년 10월 23일 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin nasceu aproximadamente 1165, em Brehna, Kreis Bitterfeld, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, Alemanha, seu pai, Friedrich von Brehna I, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Hedvika Přemyslovna, tinha 12 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Jutta von Ziegenhain. Ele faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1221, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin родился около 1165 г. в местности брэна, крис биттирфелд, провинз сачсен, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Friedrich von Brehna I было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Hedvika Přemyslovna, 12. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Jutta von Ziegenhain. Он умер 23 октября 1221 г. в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Friedrich II von Brehna und Wettin nació aproximadamente 1165, en Brehna, Kreis Bitterfeld, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, Alemania, su papá, Friedrich von Brehna I, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Hedvika Přemyslovna, tenía 12 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Jutta von Ziegenhain. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1221, a la edad de 57 años.
